name: Ghost
author: TNN1T1S
description: Ghost is a Rack-native drum system inspired by classic 909 behavior: separate voices, shared accent, and a central controller that makes the kit behave like one instrument.

modules.name: GHOST KCK
modules.description: 909-inspired kick voice. Struck bridged-T resonator model with damped sine body, pitch-envelope glide, bandpassed click transient, and tanh drive. Works standalone; comes alive with GHOST CTRL.

modules.name: GHOST SNR
modules.description: 909-inspired snare voice. Tune/Tone/Snappy/Level surface over reset triangle VCOs, fixed binary noise, and split body/noise envelopes. Accent-aware via GHOST CTRL.

modules.name: GHOST OHCH
modules.description: Combined open/closed hi-hat voice. CH and OH share a coupled sound path: a CH hit instantly mutes any sounding OH, reproducing the canonical hi-hat choke behavior often mistaken for compression. Accent-aware via GHOST CTRL.

modules.name: GHOST RIMCLAP
modules.description: Dual 909-style ROMpler voice: rim and clap in one module with independent triggers, controls, and outputs. Accent-aware via GHOST CTRL.

modules.name: GHOST TOMS
modules.description: 909-inspired tom kit. Three voices -- low, mid, and high -- share one calibrated engine with per-voice tune, decay, level, trigger, and output. Accent-aware via GHOST CTRL.

modules.name: GHOST CRSHRIDE
modules.description: 909-inspired cymbal pair. Crash and ride share a paired module with independent Tune, Decay, Level, trigger, and output controls. Accent-aware via GHOST CTRL.

modules.name: GHOST CTRL
modules.description: Global state controller for the Ghost drum system. DEFAULT, ACCENT A, ACCENT B, and MASTER controls are broadcast to adjacent Ghost voices via the expander path. Hit-time events such as trigger and local/total accent gates are patched directly to each voice. Use GHOST CTRL to make the separate voices behave like one instrument.

modules.name: GHOST MIX
modules.description: Dedicated summing mixer for the Ghost drum kit. Twelve labeled inputs -- one per voice of the full kit (Kick, Snare, Rim, Clap, Tom Lo/Mid/Hi, Closed Hat, Open Hat, Crash, Ride) plus a MIXIN channel (the 909's external mix-in) -- each with a mute switch, summed to one MIX output. Per-voice level lives on the voices, so the mixer is a clean unity summer -- the whole kit on one master mix point, in the box.
